utility_evaluation.REASONING: First, count the number of items in the required format: 1) health_coverage, 2) property_coverage, 3) travel_coverage, 4) additional_services, 5) total_budget — so total 5 items. Next, check the final coverage plan: It contains 'health_coverage' with all family members listed, plan names, costs, and coverage details (includes both physical and mental health aspects through preventive care and mental health support). 'property_coverage' is present with plan names, costs, and coverage details for different family members. 'travel_coverage' is present with plan names, costs, and coverage details. 'additional_services' is present with enhancement services and relevant details, though some costs are not explicitly listed for each service, but the requirement is 'any enhancement services with costs'. Since costs for these additional services are not clearly specified per service, this is incomplete. 'total_budget' is present with monthly and annual cost breakdown and savings. Therefore, 4 items are fully specified, and 1 item (additional_services) is missing required cost details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: First, identify each unique plan name in the final coverage plan and match it exactly to the corresponding entry in the ground-truth ratings. For health_coverage, 'EliteCare Premium Plan' and 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' appear. In the ground truth, both have ratings under primary_health_coverage -> physical_health (and the same rating for mental health, but we only need one mapping per unique plan). EliteCare Premium Plan has rating 10, LifeFirst Standard Plan has rating 8. For property_coverage, 'EliteHome Premium Coverage' has rating 10 and 'HomeFirst Standard Coverage' has rating 7. For travel_coverage, 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' has rating 10 and 'TravelFirst Standard Plan' has rating 8. Additional services are descriptive and not tied to a specific ground-truth plan name, so no ratings are mapped for them.
